mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output, which signals robust economic activity and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as corporate earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between INDPRO changes and 20-day SPX returns is not positive over any 2-year rolling window; or average return during rising INDPRO periods is not higher than during declining periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0%, it signals a shift toward labor market weakness that reduces aggregate demand and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered months over a 3-year rolling window, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is positive; or the average return after trigger is not statistically different from zero (one-sided t-test at 5% significance).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts (HOUST) and a high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 5% indicates tightening financial conditions and a weakening housing sector, which propagates to the broader economy and depresses equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 20-day SPX return following the joint trigger is positive over any 5-year window; or the signal does not produce a statistically significant negative average return (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) amplifies expectations of further rate hikes, increasing discount rates on equity cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The tightening cycle is defined by a sustained rise in FEDFUNDS.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles, the correlation between MoM changes in CPIAUCSL and 20-day SPX returns is not negative over any 2-year rolling window; or the average 20-day return during rising CPI months in tightening cycles is not lower than during falling CPI months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals immediate labor market strength and consumer spending power, providing a direct boost to corporate earnings that is quickly priced into equity markets over the next 5 trading days, leading to positive returns.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return correlation with PAYEMS changes is non-positive over any 1-year rolling window; or the average 5-day return in months with rising PAYEMS is not higher than in months with declining PAYEMS (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above a 0.5% sequential threshold reflects sustained economic momentum that supports corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days, as investors extrapolate the expansion.
failure_condition: The average 60-day SPX return following GDP growth above the threshold is zero or negative over any 10-year period; or the hit rate (positive returns) is below 50% over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and increase in unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a deterioration in consumer health and rising economic slack, which reduces corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the slowdown materializes.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month and UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: The average 60-day SPX return following the joint trigger is positive over any 5-year window; or the negative return is not statistically significant at the 5% level over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 5.25%, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance that immediately tightens financial conditions and increases the cost of capital, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity markets reprice.
failure_condition: The proportion of positive 5-day SPX returns following the trigger exceeds 50% over any 1-year rolling window; or the average 5-day return following the trigger is not negative over any 5-year window.
